assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions job termination and slice restarts but doesn't specifically explain that TPU slices are recreated atomically rather than through rolling upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response states 'Even if maintenance targets specific slices, the entire multislice job typically fails since it requires all slices to be healthy'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Complete job termination: Your training job will be killed, not gracefully paused' and mentions simultaneous restart in recovery section

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Variable downtime: Maintenance windows can last 30 minutes to several hours' but doesn't specifically explain the blocking behavior when insufficient resources are available

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response recommends 'Force a checkpoint ~30 minutes before the window' in the best practices section

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed implementation for automatic resume including 'resume_training()' function and 'ENABLE_AUTO_RESUME' environment variable

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the option to defer maintenance events

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response mentions monitoring maintenance windows but doesn't suggest using them to schedule events during planned breaks in training
